{"compiler":{"version":"0.8.19+commit.7dd6d404"},"language":"Solidity","output":{"abi":[{"inputs":[{"internalType":"string","name":"_initialPerpContractAddress","type":"string"},{"internalType":"uint256","name":"_timelockDelay","type":"uint256"}],"stateMutability":"nonpayable","type":"constructor"},{"anonymous":false,"inputs":[{"indexed":true,"internalType":"address","name":"sender","type":"address"},{"indexed":false,"internalType":"uint256","name":"erc20Amount","type":"uint256"},{"indexed":false,"internalType":"string","name":"vaultAddress","type":"string"},{"indexed":false,"internalType":"string","name":"bankDenom","type":"string"},{"indexed":false,"internalType":"address","name":"bankAddress","type":"address"}],"name":"DepositCollateral","type":"event"},{"anonymous":false,"inputs":[{"indexed":true,"internalType":"address","name":"previousOwner","type":"address"},{"indexed":true,"internalType":"address","name":"newOwner","type":"address"}],"name":"OwnershipTransferred","type":"event"},{"anonymous":false,"inputs":[{"indexed":true,"internalType":"string","name":"newAddress","type":"string"},{"indexed":false,"internalType":"uint256","name":"effectiveTimestamp","type":"uint256"}],"name":"PerpContractAddressChangeProposed","type":"event"},{"anonymous":false,"inputs":[{"indexed":true,"internalType":"string","name":"oldAddress","type":"string"},{"indexed":true,"internalType":"string","name":"newAddress","type":"string"}],"name":"PerpContractAddressChanged","type":"event"},{"inputs":[],"name":"PRECISION","outputs":[{"internalType":"uint256","name":"","type":"uint256"}],"stateMutability":"view","type":"function"},{"inputs":[{"internalType":"address","name":"token","type":"address"},{"internalType":"uint256","name":"amount","type":"uint256"},{"internalType":"string","name":"recipient","type":"string"}],"name":"_performSendToBank","outputs":[{"internalType":"uint256","name":"","type":"uint256"}],"stateMutability":"nonpayable","type":"function"},{"inputs":[{"internalType":"st nternalType":"uint256","name":"collateralId","type":"uint256"}],"stateMutability":"view","type":"function"},{"inputs":[{"internalType":"uint256","name":"tokenIndex","type":"uint256"}],"name":"getDenomOfCollateralIndex","outputs":[{"internalType":"string","name":"denom","type":"string"}],"stateMutability":"view","type":"function"},{"inputs":[{"internalType":"uint256","name":"tokenIndex","type":"uint256"}],"name":"getErc20AddressOfCollateralIndex","outputs":[{"internalType":"address","name":"","type":"address"}],"stateMutability":"view","type":"function"},{"inputs":[{"internalType":"string","name":"_vaultAddress","type":"string"},{"internalType":"bytes","name":"wasmMsgExecute","type":"bytes"},{"internalType":"uint256","name":"withdrawAmount","type":"uint256"},{"internalType":"uint256","name":"useErc20Amount","type":"uint256"}],"name":"makeWithdrawRequest","outputs":[],"stateMutability":"nonpayable","type":"function"},{"inputs":[{"internalType":"bytes","name":"wasmMsgExecute","type":"bytes"},{"internalType":"uint256","name":"collateralIndex","type":"uint256"},{"internalType":"uint256","name":"depositAmount","type":"uint256"},{"internalType":"uint256","name":"useERC20Amount","type":"uint256"}],"name":"openTrade","outputs":[],"stateMutability":"nonpayable","type":"function"},{"inputs":[],"name":"owner","outputs":[{"internalType":"address","name":"","type":"address"}],"stateMutability":"view","type":"function"},{"inputs":[],"name":"pendingPerpContractAddress","outputs":[{"internalType":"string","name":"","type":"string"}],"stateMutability":"view","type":"function"},{"inputs":[],"name":"perpContractAddress","outputs":[{"internalType":"string","name":"","type":"string"}],"stateMutability":"view","type":"function"},{"inputs":[{"internalType":"string","name":"_newAddress","type":"string"}],"name":"proposeNewPerpContractAddress","outputs":[],"stateMutability":"nonpayable","type":"function"},{"inputs":[{"internalType":"bytes","name":"wasmMsgExecute","type":"bytes"},{"internalType":"string","name":"_vaultAddress","type":"string"},{"internalType":"uint256","name":"redeemAmount","type":"uint256"},{"internalType":"bool","name":"sendCollateralToEvm","type":"bool"}],"name":"redeem","outputs":[],"stateMutability":"nonpayable","type":"function"},{"inputs":[],"name":"renounceOwnership","outputs":[],"stateMutability":"nonpayable","type":"function"},{"inputs":[],"name":"timelockDelay","outputs":[{"internalType":"uint256","name":"","type":"uint256"}],"stateMutability":"view","type":"function"},{"inputs":[{"internalType":"address","name":"newOwner","type":"address"}],"name":"transferOwnership","outputs":[],"stateMutability":"nonpayable","type":"function"},{"inputs":[{"internalType":"uint256","name":"tradeIndex","type":"uint256"},{"internalType":"uint256","name":"newLeverage","type":"uint256"},{"internalType":"uint256","name":"collateralIndex","type":"uint256"},{"internalType":"uint256","name":"collateralAmount","type":"uint256"},{"internalType":"uint256","name":"useErc20Amount","type":"uint256"}],"name":"updateLeverage","outputs":[],"stateMutability":"nonpayable","type":"function"},{"inputs":[{"internalType":"bytes","name":"wasmMsgUpdateTp","type":"bytes"},{"internalType":"bytes","name":"wasmMsgUpdateSl","type":"bytes"},{"internalType":"uint256","name":"tradeIndex","type":"uint256"},{"internalType":"uint256","name":"newLeverage","type":"uint256"},{"internalType":"uint256","name":"collateralIndex","type":"uint256"},{"internalType":"uint256","name":"collateralAmount","type":"uint256"},{"internalType":"uint256","name":"useErc20Amount","type":"uint256"}],"name":"updateTpAndLeverage","outputs":[],"stateMutability":"nonpayable","type":"function"}],"devdoc":{"kind":"dev","methods":{"claimReferrerRewardsToEvm(uint256[])":{"details":"The caller must provide the collateral indices they expect to receive rewards in.      The function:        1) Records pre-claim BANK balances for each denom.        2) Executes {\"claim_referrer_fees\":{}} on the Perp contract.        3) Bridges any balance increases back to the EVM address via `sendToEvm`.","params":{"collateralIndices":"List of collateral indices to check for rewards (e.g., [0,1])."}},"deposit(bytes,uint256,uint256,string,address,bool)":{"details":"This function facilitates depositing collateral, which can be sourced from native bank tokens      or ERC20 tokens. It interacts with a Wasm-based vault. If specified, it can also      transfer the resulting vault shares from the user's bank account to their EVM address.      The `wasmMsgExecute` parameter is crucial as it contains the specific Wasm message      (e.g., `{\"deposit\":{\"amount\":\"<depositAmount>\"}}`) required by the target vault contract      to process the deposit. The `depositAmount` parameter of this function is used to specify      the funds sent alongside the Wasm message. If the Wasm message itself contains an amount field,      it should be consistent with this `depositAmount`.      Workflow:      1. Validates that `depositAmount` is greater than zero.      2. If `sendSharesToEvm` is true or `useErc20Amount > 0`, retrieves the sender's associated Nibiru addresses.      3. If `sendSharesToEvm` is true:         a. Queries the vault (`_vaultAddress`) for its shares denomination.         b. Records the sender's current balance of these shares in their bank account.      4. Retrieves the bank denomination corresponding to the `collateralAddress` (ERC20 token).      5. If `useErc20Amount > 0`, transfers this amount of ERC20 tokens from `msg.sender` to their bank account,         converting them to their bank denomination.      6. Prepares the funds (consisting of `depositAmount` of the collateral's bank_denom) to be sent with the Wasm execution.      7. Executes the Wasm message (`wasmMsgExecute`) on the `_vaultAddress` with the prepared funds. This performs the actual deposit into the Wasm vault.      8. If `sendSharesToEvm` is true:         a. Retrieves the sender's balance of vault shares in their bank account after the deposit.         b. If the balance has increased, calculates the difference (newly minted shares).         c. Transfers these new shares from the sender's bank account to their EVM address (`msg.sender`).      9. Emits a `DepositCollateral` event.","params":{"_vaultAddress":"The string representation of the Wasm vault contract address (e.g., a Bech32 address).","collateralAddress":"The EVM address of the ERC20 token to be used as collateral.","depositAmount":"The total amount of collateral (denominated in the collateral's native bank token, e.g., uNUSD or uATOM) to be deposited into the vault.                      This amount is sent as `funds` with the `wasmMsgExecute` call.","sendSharesToEvm":"A boolean flag. If true, any vault shares minted as a result of the deposit                        will be transferred from the user's bank account to their EVM address (`msg.sender`).                        If false, shares remain in the user's bank account.","useErc20Amount":"The amount of ERC20 tokens (from `collateralAddress`) to be used for the deposit.                       If this value is greater than 0, these ERC20 tokens are first transferred from the sender's                       EVM address to their bank account. The `depositAmount` should reflect the total intended deposit,                       inclusive of any amount sourced from ERC20s.","wasmMsgExecute":"The Wasm execute message (as bytes) to be sent to the vault contract for processing the deposit.                       This message must conform to the target vault's API for deposits.                       Example: `bytes('{\"deposit\":{\"collateral\":{\"amount\":\"1000000\",\"denom\":\"unibi\"}}}')` or `bytes('{\"deposit\":{}}')` if amount is passed via funds."}},"executeNewPerpContractAddress()":{"details":"Can only be called by the owner after the timelock period has passed."},"executeSimpleFunctions(bytes)":{"details":"This function is a gateway to execute specific Wasm messages on the Perp contract. It is designed for operations that modify state but do not require sending or receiving funds. Examples of such operations include: - PERP:   - UpdateOpenLimitOrder   - UpdateTp   - CreateReferrerCode   - RedeemReferrerCode   - closeTrade","params":{"wasmMsgExecute":"The Wasm message to be executed, encoded as bytes."}},"getCollateralErc20AddressOfVault(string)":{"params":{"_vaultAddress":"The Bech32 address of the vault Wasm contract."},"returns":{"collateralAddress":"The ERC20 address of the collateral token."}},"getCollateralIndexOfTrade(string,uint256)":{"params":{"tradeId":"The index of the trade.","userBech32Address":"The bech32 address of the trader."},"returns":{"collateralId":"The numerical ID of the collateral token."}},"getErc20AddressOfCollateralIndex(uint256)":{"params":{"tokenIndex":"The index of the collateral token in the perp contract."},"returns":{"_0":"The ERC20 address of the collateral token."}},"makeWithdrawRequest(string,bytes,uint256,uint256)":{"params":{"_vaultAddress":"Bech32 Wasm vault address.","useErc20Amount":"If > 0, number of share ERC-20 tokens to first bridge into BANK.","wasmMsgExecute":"JSON-encoded execute msg for the vault\u2019s `make_withdraw_request` entry-point.","withdrawAmount":"Amount of shares (BANK units) to withdraw/lock."}},"openTrade(bytes,uint256,uint256,uint256)":{"details":"This function allows a user to open a trade by specifying the Wasm message for the      perpetuals contract, the collateral to deposit for the operation,      and an optional amount      of ERC20 tokens to first transfer to their bank balance.      It first determines the collateral address based on `collateralIndex`.      If `depositAmount` and `useERC20Amount` are greater than zero, it transfers that amount of the specified      ERC20 collateral from `msg.sender` to their corresponding bank module address.      It then checks if the `msg.sender` has sufficient balance of the collateral in the bank module      to cover the `depositAmount`.      Finally, it constructs the funds array and calls `_performWasmExecute` to send the      `wasmMsgExecute` message along with the `depositAmount` as funds to the `perpContractAddress`.      IMPORTANT: The wasmMsgExecute should include \"is_evm_origin\": true to ensure that      funds are properly returned to the EVM address when the trade is closed. Frontends      may also include \"client_origin\" in the Wasm message for public attribution.Requirements:      - If `depositAmount` is greater than 0, the `msg.sender` must have a bank balance of the specified collateral        greater than or equal to `depositAmount` (after any `useERC20Amount` transfer).","params":{"collateralIndex":"The index used to identify the collateral token.","depositAmount":"The amount of collateral (in its native bank denomination) to attach and deposit.                      The trade spend is specified by the Wasm message's collateral_amount field.","useERC20Amount":"The amount of ERC20 collateral to transfer from the user's ERC20 balance                       to their bank module balance before opening the trade. If depositAmount is 0, this is ignored.                       This is useful if the user holds collateral as ERC20s and needs to move it to the                       bank module to be used by the perpetuals contract.","wasmMsgExecute":"The Wasm execute message (e.g., for opening a position) to be sent to the perpetuals contract.                       Must include \"is_evm_origin\": true for proper fund handling. May include                       \"client_origin\" (for example \"sai_web\" or a partner slug) for attribution."}},"owner()":{"details":"Returns the address of the current owner."},"proposeNewPerpContractAddress(string)":{"details":"Can only be called by the owner. Starts the timelock period.","params":{"_newAddress":"The proposed new Bech32 address for the Perp contract."}},"redeem(bytes,string,uint256,bool)":{"params":{"_vaultAddress":"Bech32 Wasm vault address.","redeemAmount":"Amount of vault shares (in BANK units) to redeem.","sendCollateralToEvm":"If true, any newly-received collateral is bridged back to the EVM.","wasmMsgExecute":"JSON-encoded execute msg for the vault\u2019s `redeem` entry-point."}},"renounceOwnership()":{"details":"Leaves the contract without owner. It will not be possible to call `onlyOwner` functions. Can only be called by the current owner. NOTE: Renouncing ownership will leave the contract without an owner, thereby disabling any functionality that is only available to the owner."},"transferOwnership(address)":{"details":"Transfers ownership of the contract to a new account (`newOwner`). Can only be called by the current owner."},"updateTpAndLeverage(bytes,bytes,uint256,uint256,uint256,uint256,uint256)":{"details":"Executes two operations: first UpdateTp, then delegates to updateLeverage.","params":{"collateralAmount":"Amount of collateral to send (only for leverage decrease).","collateralIndex":"The index of the collateral token (for leverage update).","newLeverage":"The new leverage value (must be within min/max bounds).","tradeIndex":"The index of the trade to update leverage for.","useErc20Amount":"Amount of ERC20 to convert before updating leverage.","wasmMsgUpdateSl":"The Wasm message for updating stop loss, encoded as bytes.","wasmMsgUpdateTp":"The Wasm message for updating take profit, encoded as bytes."}}},"version":1},"userdoc":{"kind":"user","methods":{"claimReferrerRewardsToEvm(uint256[])":{"notice":"Claims accumulated referrer fees and bridges them back to the caller as ERC-20s."},"deposit(bytes,uint256,uint256,string,address,bool)":{"notice":"Deposits collateral into a specified vault."},"executeNewPerpContractAddress()":{"notice":"Executes the proposed change to the Perp contract address."},"executeSimpleFunctions(bytes)":{"notice":"Executes Perp Wasm messages that do not involve fund transfers."},"executeVaultSimpleFunctions(bytes,string)":{"notice":"- CancelWithdrawRequest   - UnlockDeposit"},"getCollateralErc20AddressOfVault(string)":{"notice":"Gets the ERC20 address corresponding to the vault's collateral denom."},"getCollateralIndexOfTrade(string,uint256)":{"notice":"Retrieves the collateral index (TokenIndex) for a specific trade."},"getErc20AddressOfCollateralIndex(uint256)":{"notice":"Gets the ERC20 address corresponding to a perp collateral index."},"makeWithdrawRequest(string,bytes,uint256,uint256)":{"notice":"Submit a withdraw request by sending shares to the vault (timelocked unlock).         If the caller holds share ERC-20s on the EVM side, they can first convert         them back into BANK tokens via `useErc20Amount`."},"openTrade(bytes,uint256,uint256,uint256)":{"notice":"Opens a new trade on the perpetuals market."},"proposeNewPerpContractAddress(string)":{"notice":"Proposes a new Perp contract address."},"redeem(bytes,string,uint256,bool)":{"notice":"Redeem (withdraw) vault shares for the underlying collateral."},"updateTpAndLeverage(bytes,bytes,uint256,uint256,uint256,uint256,uint256)":{"notice":"Updates both take profit, stop loss and leverage in a single transaction."}},"version":1}},"settings":{"compilationTarget":{"contracts/SaiEvm.sol":"SaiEvm"},"evmVersion":"paris","libraries":{},"metadata":{"bytecodeHash":"ipfs"},"optimizer":{"enabled":true,"runs":200},"remappings":[":@nibiruchain/solidity/=node_modules/@nibiruchain/solidity/",":@openzeppelin/=node_modules/@openzeppelin/",":eth-gas-reporter/=node_modules/eth-gas-reporter/",":hardhat/=node_modules/hardhat/"]},"sources":{"contracts/SaiEvm.sol":{"keccak256":"0x82b26235836ce84cb0505dc5023c5126139f5a9b7f1fe0ed039026f3e940d893","license":"UNLICENSED","urls":["bzz-raw://40d97b5c8ad02dc67d379b6eba16fed14c5bbeaf8e82f7f94262408e0a4ce555","dweb:/ipfs/QmQLe2wLex7Ym5UvXoPN8AXeKbFVLdaVVHWqhhA3KdoPen"]},"node_modules/@nibiruchain/solidity/contracts/IFunToken.sol":{"keccak256":"0xe60751d6e9caf20c041b07bd2d1f62aefa26ced00ee83bb545c6146a690bd78b","license":"MIT","urls":["bzz-raw://7559c8bc9bb58c1dc27fb4a7bd2780325d43f748911d85fe449b8454cfdbc248","dweb:/ipfs/QmSmvWsQdziLtGvnxTDm7YKFxF1tpBdgeCMCsjAxKCJQFW"]},"node_modules/@nibiruchain/solidity/contracts/NibiruEvmUtils.sol":{"keccak256":"0x1a65ecce7e369e6f837989535986d88c1994c47c88f22df3cdffb5a325fc0e03","license":"MIT","urls":["bzz-raw://79820c86f98308007c428a8ee978118a825125f99b221d4367e482b7f4b289cb","dweb:/ipfs/QmfFjJjExFMudj5kXGuJM5iTp9F275sESDmp3V2zZuL8DL"]},"node_modules/@nibiruchain/solidity/contracts/Wasm.sol":{"keccak256":"0xce8ec481e763d3c9c53782ce9c72ca54373e6c477352e6e978845483f99634ac","license":"MIT","urls":["bzz-raw://3f1c78b1bd600c423b0575f5d35b7accfd82b08df701e3300b1ce30c940288cd","dweb:/ipfs/QmPAucihLbPHwY8WVkYfH8br8gTkAyM79cQzAMMSbcAjfE"]},"node_modules/@openzeppelin/contracts/access/Ownable.sol":{"keccak256":"0xba43b97fba0d32eb4254f6a5a297b39a19a247082a02d6e69349e071e2946218","license":"MIT","urls":["bzz-raw://fc980984badf3984b6303b377711220e067722bbd6a135b24669ff5069ef9f32","dweb:/ipfs/QmPHXMSXj99XjSVM21YsY6aNtLLjLVXDbyN76J5HQYvvrz"]},"node_modules/@openzeppelin/contracts/token/ERC20/IERC20.sol":{"keccak256":"0x287b55befed2961a7eabd7d7b1b2839cbca8a5b80ef8dcbb25ed3d4c2002c305","license":"MIT","urls":["bzz-raw://bd39944e8fc06be6dbe2dd1d8449b5336e23c6a7ba3e8e9ae5ae0f37f35283f5","dweb:/ipfs/QmPV3FGYjVwvKSgAXKUN3r9T9GwniZz83CxBpM7vyj2G53"]},"node_modules/@openzeppelin/contracts/utils/Context.sol":{"keccak256":"0xa92e4fa126feb6907daa0513ddd816b2eb91f30a808de54f63c17d0e162c3439","license":"MIT","urls":["bzz-raw://a367861093b74443b137564d3f3c472f70bcf114739e62059c939f25e315706c","dweb:/ipfs/Qmd7JMpcxD9RuQjK3uM3EzJUgSqdN8vzp8eytEiuwxQJ6h"]},"node_modules/@openzeppelin/contracts/utils/Strings.sol":{"keccak256":"0x3088eb2868e8d13d89d16670b5f8612c4ab9ff8956272837d8e90106c59c14a0","license":"MIT","urls":["bzz-raw://b81d9ff6559ea5c47fc573e17ece6d9ba5d6839e213e6ebc3b4c5c8fe4199d7f","dweb:/ipfs/QmPCW1bFisUzJkyjroY3yipwfism9RRCigCcK1hbXtVM8n"]},"node_modules/@openzeppelin/contracts/utils/math/Math.sol":{"keccak256":"0xe4455ac1eb7fc497bb7402579e7b4d64d928b846fce7d2b6fde06d366f21c2b3","license":"MIT","urls":["bzz-raw://cc8841b3cd48ad125e2f46323c8bad3aa0e88e399ec62acb9e57efa7e7c8058c","dweb:/ipfs/QmSqE4mXHA2BXW58deDbXE8MTcsL5JSKNDbm23sVQxRLPS"]},"node_modules/@openzeppelin/contracts/utils/math/SignedMath.sol":{"keccak256":"0xf92515413956f529d95977adc9b0567d583c6203fc31ab1c23824c35187e3ddc","license":"MIT","urls":["bzz-raw://c50fcc459e49a9858b6d8ad5f911295cb7c9ab57567845a250bf0153f84a95c7","dweb:/ipfs/QmcEW85JRzvDkQggxiBBLVAasXWdkhEysqypj9EaB6H2g6"]}},"version":1}

claimReferrerRewardsToEvm(uint256[]).details: The caller must provide the collateral indices they expect to receive rewards in.      The function:        1) Records pre-claim BANK balances for each denom.        2) Executes {"claim_referrer_fees":{}} on the Perp contract.        3) Bridges any balance increases back to the EVM address via `sendToEvm`.

params.collateralIndices: List of collateral indices to check for rewards (e.g., [0,1]).

deposit(bytes,uint256,uint256,string,add.details: This function facilitates depositing collateral, which can be sourced from native bank tokens      or ERC20 tokens. It interacts with a Wasm-based vault. If specified, it can also      transfer the resulting vault shares from the user's bank account to their EVM address.      The `wasmMsgExecute` parameter is crucial as it contains the specific Wasm message      (e.g., `{"deposit":{"amount":"<depositAmount>"}}`) required by the target vault contract      to process the deposit. The `depositAmount` parameter of this function is used to specify      the funds sent alongside the Wasm message. If the Wasm message itself contains an amount field,      it should be consistent with this `depositAmount`.      Workflow:      1. Validates that `depositAmount` is greater than zero.      2. If `sendSharesToEvm` is true or `useErc20Amount > 0`, retrieves the sender's associated Nibiru addresses.      3. If `sendSharesToEvm` is true:         a. Queries the vault (`_vaultAddress`) for its shares denomination.         b. Records the sender's current balance of these shares in their bank account.      4. Retrieves the bank denomination corresponding to the `collateralAddress` (ERC20 token).      5. If `useErc20Amount > 0`, transfers this amount of ERC20 tokens from `msg.sender` to their bank account,         converting them to their bank denomination.      6. Prepares the funds (consisting of `depositAmount` of the collateral's bank_denom) to be sent with the Wasm execution.      7. Executes the Wasm message (`wasmMsgExecute`) on the `_vaultAddress` with the prepared funds. This performs the actual deposit into the Wasm vault.      8. If `sendSharesToEvm` is true:         a. Retrieves the sender's balance of vault shares in their bank account after the deposit.         b. If the balance has increased, calculates the difference (newly minted shares).         c. Transfers these new shares from the sender's bank account to their EVM address (`msg.sender`).      9. Emits a `DepositCollateral` event.

params._vaultAddress: The string representation of the Wasm vault contract address (e.g., a Bech32 address).
params.collateralAddress: The EVM address of the ERC20 token to be used as collateral.
params.depositAmount: The total amount of collateral (denominated in the collateral's native bank token, e.g., uNUSD or uATOM) to be deposited into the vault.                      This amount is sent as `funds` with the `wasmMsgExecute` call.
params.sendSharesToEvm: A boolean flag. If true, any vault shares minted as a result of the deposit                        will be transferred from the user's bank account to their EVM address (`msg.sender`).                        If false, shares remain in the user's bank account.
params.useErc20Amount: The amount of ERC20 tokens (from `collateralAddress`) to be used for the deposit.                       If this value is greater than 0, these ERC20 tokens are first transferred from the sender's                       EVM address to their bank account. The `depositAmount` should reflect the total intended deposit,                       inclusive of any amount sourced from ERC20s.
params.wasmMsgExecute: The Wasm execute message (as bytes) to be sent to the vault contract for processing the deposit.                       This message must conform to the target vault's API for deposits.                       Example: `bytes('{"deposit":{"collateral":{"amount":"1000000","denom":"unibi"}}}')` or `bytes('{"deposit":{}}')` if amount is passed via funds.

executeNewPerpContractAddress().details: Can only be called by the owner after the timelock period has passed.

executeSimpleFunctions(bytes).details: This function is a gateway to execute specific Wasm messages on the Perp contract. It is designed for operations that modify state but do not require sending or receiving funds. Examples of such operations include: - PERP:   - UpdateOpenLimitOrder   - UpdateTp   - CreateReferrerCode   - RedeemReferrerCode   - closeTrade

params.wasmMsgExecute: The Wasm message to be executed, encoded as bytes.

params._vaultAddress: The Bech32 address of the vault Wasm contract.

returns.collateralAddress: The ERC20 address of the collateral token.

params.userBech32Address: The bech32 address of the trader.

returns.collateralId: The numerical ID of the collateral token.

params.tokenIndex: The index of the collateral token in the perp contract.

returns._0: The ERC20 address of the collateral token.

params.useErc20Amount: If > 0, number of share ERC-20 tokens to first bridge into BANK.
params.wasmMsgExecute: JSON-encoded execute msg for the vault’s `make_withdraw_request` entry-point.
params.withdrawAmount: Amount of shares (BANK units) to withdraw/lock.

openTrade(bytes,uint256,uint256,uint256).details: This function allows a user to open a trade by specifying the Wasm message for the      perpetuals contract, the collateral to deposit for the operation,      and an optional amount      of ERC20 tokens to first transfer to their bank balance.      It first determines the collateral address based on `collateralIndex`.      If `depositAmount` and `useERC20Amount` are greater than zero, it transfers that amount of the specified      ERC20 collateral from `msg.sender` to their corresponding bank module address.      It then checks if the `msg.sender` has sufficient balance of the collateral in the bank module      to cover the `depositAmount`.      Finally, it constructs the funds array and calls `_performWasmExecute` to send the      `wasmMsgExecute` message along with the `depositAmount` as funds to the `perpContractAddress`.      IMPORTANT: The wasmMsgExecute should include "is_evm_origin": true to ensure that      funds are properly returned to the EVM address when the trade is closed. Frontends      may also include "client_origin" in the Wasm message for public attribution.Requirements:      - If `depositAmount` is greater than 0, the `msg.sender` must have a bank balance of the specified collateral        greater than or equal to `depositAmount` (after any `useERC20Amount` transfer).

params.collateralIndex: The index used to identify the collateral token.
params.depositAmount: The amount of collateral (in its native bank denomination) to attach and deposit.                      The trade spend is specified by the Wasm message's collateral_amount field.
params.useERC20Amount: The amount of ERC20 collateral to transfer from the user's ERC20 balance                       to their bank module balance before opening the trade. If depositAmount is 0, this is ignored.                       This is useful if the user holds collateral as ERC20s and needs to move it to the                       bank module to be used by the perpetuals contract.
params.wasmMsgExecute: The Wasm execute message (e.g., for opening a position) to be sent to the perpetuals contract.                       Must include "is_evm_origin": true for proper fund handling. May include                       "client_origin" (for example "sai_web" or a partner slug) for attribution.

owner().details: Returns the address of the current owner.

proposeNewPerpContractAddress(string).details: Can only be called by the owner. Starts the timelock period.

params._newAddress: The proposed new Bech32 address for the Perp contract.

params.redeemAmount: Amount of vault shares (in BANK units) to redeem.
params.sendCollateralToEvm: If true, any newly-received collateral is bridged back to the EVM.
params.wasmMsgExecute: JSON-encoded execute msg for the vault’s `redeem` entry-point.

renounceOwnership().details: Leaves the contract without owner. It will not be possible to call `onlyOwner` functions. Can only be called by the current owner. NOTE: Renouncing ownership will leave the contract without an owner, thereby disabling any functionality that is only available to the owner.

transferOwnership(address).details: Transfers ownership of the contract to a new account (`newOwner`). Can only be called by the current owner.

updateTpAndLeverage(bytes,bytes,uint256,.details: Executes two operations: first UpdateTp, then delegates to updateLeverage.

params.collateralAmount: Amount of collateral to send (only for leverage decrease).
params.collateralIndex: The index of the collateral token (for leverage update).
params.newLeverage: The new leverage value (must be within min/max bounds).
params.tradeIndex: The index of the trade to update leverage for.
params.useErc20Amount: Amount of ERC20 to convert before updating leverage.
params.wasmMsgUpdateSl: The Wasm message for updating stop loss, encoded as bytes.
params.wasmMsgUpdateTp: The Wasm message for updating take profit, encoded as bytes.

claimReferrerRewardsToEvm(uint256[]).notice: Claims accumulated referrer fees and bridges them back to the caller as ERC-20s.

deposit(bytes,uint256,uint256,string,add.notice: Deposits collateral into a specified vault.

executeNewPerpContractAddress().notice: Executes the proposed change to the Perp contract address.

executeSimpleFunctions(bytes).notice: Executes Perp Wasm messages that do not involve fund transfers.

getCollateralErc20AddressOfVault(string).notice: Gets the ERC20 address corresponding to the vault's collateral denom.

getCollateralIndexOfTrade(string,uint256.notice: Retrieves the collateral index (TokenIndex) for a specific trade.

getErc20AddressOfCollateralIndex(uint256.notice: Gets the ERC20 address corresponding to a perp collateral index.

makeWithdrawRequest(string,bytes,uint256.notice: Submit a withdraw request by sending shares to the vault (timelocked unlock).         If the caller holds share ERC-20s on the EVM side, they can first convert         them back into BANK tokens via `useErc20Amount`.

openTrade(bytes,uint256,uint256,uint256).notice: Opens a new trade on the perpetuals market.

proposeNewPerpContractAddress(string).notice: Proposes a new Perp contract address.

redeem(bytes,string,uint256,bool).notice: Redeem (withdraw) vault shares for the underlying collateral.

updateTpAndLeverage(bytes,bytes,uint256,.notice: Updates both take profit, stop loss and leverage in a single transaction.
